history.content:
<thought>
I am facing north. The cell directly ahead is a floor cell (.), safe to move. The goal is two cells north and two cells east. I plan to move north twice, then turn right and move east twice to reach the goal. The immediate forward move is safe.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row7 col3 facing north. The cell directly ahead (north) is a floor cell (.), safe to step. The goal is located two cells east and one cell north from the current position. The immediate forward move is safe and brings me closer to the goal. After moving north, I can turn right and move east twice to reach the goal, avoiding the lava cells to the east on the current row.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (6,3) facing north. The cell directly ahead is a wall (#), so we cannot move forward. The goal G is at (6,5) to the east, with a safe floor cell (.) at (6,4). I need to turn right to face east, then move forward twice to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (7,4) facing east. The goal G is located at (5,4), two steps north, with a clear floor cell at (6,4) directly ahead in that direction. Moving east would lead to a dead end or lava, so the best path is to go north. I need to turn left to face north first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north, but the cell directly ahead is a wall (#). The goal G is located to the east, two cells away, with a floor cell (.) in between. To reach the goal, I need to turn right to face east, then move forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is located two cells north of my current position (row4, col3). The path north is clear: the cell directly north (row5, col3) is floor, and the next cell is the goal. I am currently facing east after the previous turn. To move toward the goal, I need to face north. The cell to my east is a floor but leads away. So I will turn left to face north.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom row, center column, facing north. The goal G is located two cells to the east on the same row. The cell directly to the north is a wall (#), but the east path is clear (first a floor cell, then the goal). To move toward the goal, I need to face east, so I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is located two cells directly north of my position. The cell immediately north is floor (.), which is safe. I can move forward to approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is directly in front of me (north). There are no obstacles in that cell. I can move forward to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
